title: stelis ephippium
intro: Stelis ephippium, also known as the Antelope Orchid, is a type of epiphytic orchid native to Central and South America. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including skin infections and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts have been found to have antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for wound healing and immune system support. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Stelis ephippium are typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
